Off Calendar Jul-01-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Richard S. Diestel and Lael Abaya of BLEDSOE, CATHCART, DIESTEL, PEDERSEN & TREPPA LLP (415-981-5411), appearing for defendant / moving party Del Paso Pipe & Steel, Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Betty Williams and Glenn Williams, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT DEL PASO PIPE & STEEL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Del Paso Pipe & Steel, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also did not sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiff's claim. Harlan Karnofsky's declaration is irrelevant. Abraham Karnofsky's declaration, even if admissible, merely creates a triable issue when read in conjunction with plaintiff's deposition testimony whether defendant sold, supplied or distributed sheet gasket material. Plaintiff's objections to Harlan Karnofsky's declaration based on relevance are sustained. Plaintiff's objections to Abraham Karnofsky's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara A. Lohman of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
